summary:
• Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz geopolitical risks pose critical threats to Western Hemisphere crude supply chains, with potential toll regimes and shipping disruptions capable of significantly roiling global oil markets.

• China's collapsing oil imports and demand shift toward coal alternatives indicate reduced competition for Americas crude exports, while simultaneously signaling weakening global demand that could pressure pricing leverage for Western producers.

• Russian export cutbacks driven by sanctions and infrastructure damage create supply tightness in non-Western markets, potentially redirecting Asian buyers toward Americas sources but also raising systemic oil price volatility risks that threaten downstream energy security.
